name: Punaluu
desc: Quiet windward coast village — black sand pocket beach, art gallery, remote and serene with mountain backdrops.
insider: Punaluu Beach Park is a gorgeous pocket beach with calm, clear water and almost nobody on it — one of the windward coast's best secrets
The small art gallery in town showcases local windward artists and craftspeople — definitely worth a stop if it's open
Pat's at Punaluu is the landmark condo complex — some of the most affordable beachfront vacation rentals on all of Oahu
The drive through Punaluu at sunrise with the Ko'olau cliffs lit up golden is absolutely magical — pull over and take it in
Kenui Road leads to a quiet residential area with some of the most affordable oceanfront homes in the state
The beach has a natural offshore reef that keeps the water calm — great for kids and beginner snorkelers
There are no restaurants or stores in Punaluu proper — the nearest groceries are in Hauula or Kahuku
Punaluu is one of the wettest spots on the windward coast — expect rain almost daily and lush green scenery year-round
The old Punaluu railroad bridge remnants are visible near the highway — a relic of the Oahu Railway & Land Company era
Night sky viewing here is excellent — minimal light pollution makes it one of the better stargazing spots on Oahu
